mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in INDPRO signals improving production volumes and near-term earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the trend remains above zero.
evidence_refs: fact:latest visible INDPRO level was 107.055 as of two months before 2005-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than the unconditional hit rate over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 3.5% after rising more than 75 basis points over six months, tighter discount rates reduce equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 3.5% and its 6-month change is greater than +0.75pp
evidence_refs: fact:latest visible FEDFUNDS level was 3.78 as of one month before 2005-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time or if mean triggered returns are not below unconditional mean returns.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth exceeds CPIAUCSL growth by more than 2 percentage points over three months, real consumption momentum is improving, raising expected earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month annualized RSAFS growth crosses above 3-month annualized CPIAUCSL growth by more than 2pp
evidence_refs: fact:latest visible RSAFS was 351561 and CPIAUCSL was 198.5
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction spread has nonpositive rank correlation with subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns over two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A UNRATE increase of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals weakening labor demand and rising recession risk, reducing earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest visible UNRATE level was 5.0 as of one month before 2005-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by 60-day S&P 500 returns above the unconditional median in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST crosses above 2000 thousand units and remains above its 12-month average, housing activity signals strong household balance sheets and construction demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above 2000 and is above its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:latest visible HOUST level was 2108 as of two months before 2005-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered high-HOUST periods have lower average 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered periods in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS rises over six months while UNRATE stays at or below 5.5%, tightening is occurring alongside resilient labor demand, validating earnings growth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 6-month change is positive and UNRATE is at or below 5.5%
evidence_refs: fact:latest visible FEDFUNDS was 3.78 and UNRATE was 5.0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition produces lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than periods with rising FEDFUNDS and UNRATE above 5.5% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in PAYEMS signals ongoing job creation and supports household income expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through short-horizon risk appetite.
evidence_refs: fact:latest visible PAYEMS level was 134061 as of one month before 2005-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month PAYEMS changes do not improve 5-day directional accuracy above 52% in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while INDPRO declines over three months, margin pressure and weaker output jointly signal stagflation risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month annualized CPIAUCSL growth crosses above 4% while 3-month INDPRO growth is below 0%
evidence_refs: fact:latest visible CPIAUCSL was 198.5 and INDPRO was 107.055
failure_condition: Reject if triggered stagflation-risk observations produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases or have mean returns above unconditional mean returns.
